javax.media.mscontrol
Interface Parameters

All Superinterfaces:
java.util.Map<Parameter,java.lang.Object>
public interface Parameters
extends java.util.Map<Parameter,java.lang.Object>

This class is a Map of (Parameter=value) pairs. Each key identifies a Parameter, and the Map provides its value.
Example: 

 Parameters options = myMediaGroup.createParameters();
 options.put(Recorder.AUDIO_CODEC, CodecConstants.MULAW_PCM_64K);
 myMediaGroup.getRecorder().record("/var/tmp/media/message.wav, null, options);
Note 1:The Parameters object must not be modified until the transaction is complete (the record transaction in the above example).
Note 2: The value may be an instance of Value, for an enumerated Parameter, or any other java Object, for a non-enumerated Parameter.
This is indicated in each Parameter documentation.

Nested Class Summary
 
Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface java.util.Map
java.util.Map.Entry<K,V>
 
Field Summary
static Parameters NO_PARAMETER
          A typed constant to use when no parameter is required:
play("my_file.wav, myRTCs, Parameters.NO_PARAMETER);

Field Detail

NO_PARAMETER

static final Parameters NO_PARAMETER
A typed constant to use when no parameter is required:
play("my_file.wav, myRTCs, Parameters.NO_PARAMETER);
